title: [v3.0] Use cachix for static build & include podman-remote build

comments.body: @saschagrunert PTAL...Does the cache "key" need to be altered (on this branch) somehow, so the cache is distinct from the master branch?  *note* we have the `$DEST_BRANCH` variable available for use in `.cirrus.yml` and every other script, in case that helps.

> @saschagrunert PTAL...Does the cache "key" need to be altered (on this branch) somehow, so the cache is distinct from the master branch? _note_ we have the `$DEST_BRANCH` variable available for use in `.cirrus.yml` and every other script, in case that helps.

This looks fine from my side. The cache can be re-used, even if some derivations differ. Cachix will just put both into the same cache side by side with a different checksum.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.0/OWNERS)~~ [saschagrunert] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Cachix will just put both into the same cache side by side with a different checksum.

Okay, glad to hear.  This is what I wasn't sure about.
